title: Miter Saw Cart
summary: A rolling plywood cart with fold-down leaves that support long boards level with the saw deck, plus lower shelves for storage.
description:
The single measurement that makes this cart actually useful is the cleat height: it has to sit exactly the distance down from the plywood's top edge that matches your own saw's deck height, so a board resting on the fold-out leaves sits flush with the saw table instead of tipping into it or sitting proud of it. Measure your specific saw before cutting anything — this number is not universal across brands or models.

The fold-down leaves are what let the cart double as a support table when the saw isn't in use, and also what let it collapse flat against a wall for storage. A second locking hinge support (beyond the plain shelf-hinge support) is worth adding if the leaves will also serve as an assembly table, since a single support isn't rated to hold a workpiece being pressed on or clamped.

materials.name: Plywood, 3/4", 4x8 sheet
materials.note: Ripped lengthwise into two 24" x 96" strips.

materials.name: Plate-mount caster wheels, with locking brakes
Shelf hinge supports, 12"
materials.note: A locking support is recommended if the leaves double as an assembly table.

cutList.note: Positioned to match your saw's deck height — measure before cutting.

steps.title: Mark and attach the cleats
steps.body: Measure your own saw's deck height and mark this exact distance down from the top edge of the plywood sides — this is the single most important measurement in the build, since it determines whether a board resting on the leaves sits flush with your saw table. Attach the cleats at this height.

steps.title: Attach the shelves
steps.body: Attach the shelves to the sides. Pocket holes and 1-1/4" pocket hole screws and glue, or brackets, add a little extra support beyond resting on the cleats. Extra shelves can be added if desired.

steps.title: Add the bottom trim
steps.body: Add the bottom trim, mounted flush to the bottom, to reinforce the lower joint. A lower shelf can double as scrap wood storage.

steps.title: Mount the casters
steps.body: Mount the caster wheels to the bottom of the cart.
materials: Plate-mount caster wheels, with locking brakes

steps.title: Attach the fold-down leaves
steps.body:
Attach the fold-down leaves to the sides of the cart with the shelf hinge supports, so each leaf folds flat against the cart when not needed and folds out level with the saw deck when in use.

Sand every surface and finish.

steps.title: Sand and finish
steps.body: Sand every surface smooth and finish as desired.

images.alt: Rolling miter saw cart with fold-down support leaves
